name: sgs/decorative-image
title: SGS Decorative Image
description: Absolute-positioned decorative image that floats freely over sections. Used for organic, editorial-style design with optional parallax scroll effects.

image.label: Image
image._note: An empty-string prefix does NOT trigger the default-convention resolver (it's checked with `if (element.prefix)`, and '' is falsy) — so opacity/zIndex are mapped explicitly rather than relying on bare-attribute matching. positionX/positionY are percentage-based anchor coordinates for the whole floating element, not literal CSS top/bottom offsets or object-position within a box, so they are deliberately left unmapped — css:top/css:bottom stay genuine gaps rather than being force-mapped to a different semantic. No layer field is declared (this is not a composite). The old motion cluster (transition-duration/transition-timing-function) is still deliberately NOT declared here — these are JS-driven scroll-triggered parameters, not CSS transitions, and mapping them there would misrepresent the mechanism.
image._note_animation: FR-35-6 — pathDrawDurationMs/pathDrawEasing/parallaxStrength/pathDrawOnScroll now resolve via the `animation` cluster's anim:duration/anim:easing/anim:parallax/anim:trigger members (explicit attrMap; the mechanism this block actually uses — JS scroll-observers — is exactly what FR-35-6 exists for). anim:preset and anim:stagger are honest gaps: this block has no sgsAnimation preset picker or staggerDelay attribute. pathDrawTriggerOffset and fadeOnScroll are NOT claimed by any member (pathDrawTriggerOffset is a secondary trigger-offset param with only one attrMap slot per member already used by pathDrawOnScroll; fadeOnScroll is a bare boolean fade toggle with no duration/easing of its own) — both stay unmapped; no orphan is reported for either because this element's `prefix: ""` opts it out of orphan-scanning entirely.

attributes._comment_decorMedia: Migrated 2026-05-05 — legacy imageId/imageUrl/imageAlt retained for deprecation. New instances should use decorMedia (unified image-or-video slot).
